title: ruscus procerus
intro: Ruscus procerus, also known as Butcher's Broom, is a flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include being a vasodilator, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic. The health benefits of Ruscus procerus include improved blood circulation, reduced swelling, and relief from symptoms associated with varicose veins and hemorrhoids. Ruscus procerus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
